Job Title: Senior ML Software Developer - Python & Machine Learning
Ad Text:
Senior Machine Learning Software Developer (Python)

6month + 6 months contract
Work for a global mining client in the Perth CBD
Competitive daily rates offered
Our client
A leading Australian mining organisation undertaking a major transformation of its operations, driven by automation and advanced digital optimisation. The organisation is progressing toward energy‑efficient, highly autonomous mine sites, enabled by renewable energy integration, battery‑electric equipment, and intelligent, real‑time decision‑making platforms
 
The Role
This position involves building and enhancing distributed backend systems that underpin large, business‑critical Mining applications. A key aspect of the position involves embedding machine learning capabilities into production systems, ensuring these solutions are reliable, maintainable, and operate effectively at scale.
Key responsibilities include:

Develop and deliver scalable, high-performance backend services, including microservices and distributed system components
Continuously improve platform performance, resilience, and scalability to support production demands
Provide technical oversight through code reviews and champion consistent engineering standards across the team
Work closely with architects, data specialists, DevOps teams, and stakeholders to deliver integrated solutions
Establish and maintain effective CI/CD pipelines, along with automated testing and infrastructure-as-code practices
Ensure systems are well-monitored, highly observable, and ready to perform reliably in operational environments
Support the creation of technical documentation, including system designs, architectural artefacts, and delivery plans
 
Required Experience
To succeed in this role, you should bring:

Proven experience designing and delivering large-scale, distributed backend platforms in production environments
Strong programming ability, including experience with concurrent or multithreaded development in languages such as Python, C++, C#, Rust, or similar
Hands-on experience building and deploying microservices architectures using container technologies like Docker and orchestration tools such as Kubernetes
Familiarity with event-driven and messaging-based systems, including technologies like Kafka, RabbitMQ, or MQTT
Solid understanding of performance considerations in real-time systems, including latency, efficiency, and computational trade-offs
Experience working with modern CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure automation tools, such as GitHub Actions, Terraform, or similar platforms
 
Desired Experience

Extensive experience (typically 8+ years) developing and delivering robust, production-ready software systems in complex environments
Demonstrated ability to lead, guide, or mentor engineering teams, supporting both technical delivery and team development
Strong foundation in backend engineering and distributed system design, with a focus on reliability and scalability
Exposure to high-availability or real-time environments, particularly within operational or industrial settings (experience in sectors such as mining or heavy industry is beneficial)
Proven track record of taking end-to-end ownership of solutions, from initial design and implementation through to deployment and ongoing support
 
Why Join This Project?

Opportunity to support a high-impact digital transformation initiative.
Exposure to modern architectural patterns.
Flexible working arrangements and attractive contract rates.
 
If you are interested and possess the required skills, please apply now via the link below to be considered.

Contact: Anju Ashok – (08) 9423 1408 - (Job reference: 270689)
 


Peoplebank and Leaders IT are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone belongs. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you need adjustments to the recruitment process due to your circumstances, please let us know—we’re here to support you.

Job Title: Senior Systems Engineer - MQ-28 Digital Model Ecosystem
Ad Text:
The Boeing MQ-28 Ghost Bat is a globally-significant program that will deliver a disruptive advantage to support Defence operational capabilities. Developed in partnership with the Royal Australian Air Force, it is the first military combat aircraft to be designed, manufactured and assembled in Australia in 50 years.

As we seek to rapidly iterate and integrate new skills and capabilities to the platform, Boeing Defence Australia (BDA) are seeking an experienced Systems Engineer to support the development and deployment of our Digital Model Ecosystem. You will work collaboratively across an interdisciplinary and multinational team to inform requirements and develop digital artefacts across a broad range of domains.

This role is based in Brisbane and reports into the MQ-28 DME Engineering Manager.

As an experienced Senior Systems Engineer you will:

Engage and collaborate with key internal and external stakeholders across technical engineering and specialty areas to develop and integrate innovative artefacts to enable our digital ecosystem.
Apply an interdisciplinary, collaborative approach to define use cases, delegate requirements and inform modelling and simulation capability.
Manage dependencies, assess trades and support technical design reviews in collaboration with the broader project team to ensure the engineering scope is delivered with quality and on schedule.
Coordinate, plan and conduct integration and verification testing of the MQ-28 digital ecosystem.
Demonstrate leadership in development, review and approval of engineering documentation and designs while providing mentoring of less experienced staff.
Support third party integration, working with suppliers and customers to define, refine and utilize the MQ-28 digital environment.
Manage technical risk and support identification, analysis and resolution of problem reports.
Plan and coordinate incremental capability development and delivery within an AGILE framework.

To be considered for the role you will have:

Minimum of 5 years' experience in complex technical development and integration projects
Hold Tertiary qualifications in either Aerospace, Avionics, Electrical or Mechatronics Engineering or equivalent technical experience in a Systems Engineering discipline.
Demonstrated experience in systems and software development lifecycle (essential).
Demonstrated experience in avionics system level testing in a laboratory and ground test environments (desirable).
Experience with aircraft avionics, sensors and UAV systems (desirable).
Familiarity with engineering change processes.
Familiarity with software development, test and integration.
Experience with aerospace and / or Defence projects (highly regarded).

Applicants must be Australian Citizens to meet Defence security requirements.

What's on offer?

Boeing values the health and wellbeing of our employees and offer true flexibility including working from home, compressed work weeks, and opportunities for part time arrangements, allowing you to balance your career with what is important to you outside of work.

Other benefits:

Work on cutting edge projects with opportunities to work across platforms.
Attractive remuneration and annual bonus.
Formal mentoring and training as well as on the job learning.
Formal reward and recognition program.
Access discounts for health insurance, travel and accommodation.
Paid study leave, paid parental leave and Defence leave.
Salary packaging options available.
Health and wellbeing benefits including annual flu vaccinations and Employee Assistance Program.
Social and community groups.

BDA works with strong links with our global Boeing community and we strongly encourage collaboration with our international counterparts.

We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. Female applicants, people of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island descent and veterans are encouraged to apply.

If you are ready to join an innovative industry leader and would like to register your interest in working for Boeing, please click Apply Now.

About JMC Academy

Founded in 1982, JMC Academy is Australia’s premier institution in Creative Industries education, offering a diverse range of Degrees and Diplomas in Music, Songwriting, Audio Engineering, Film and Television Production, Acting, Entertainment Business Management, Visual Communication (Design), Animation, Game Design, and Music Production.

With a 40-year legacy, JMC Academy provides cutting-edge and technologically advanced programs that meet the global demands of the creative industries. JMC operates from 3 large footprint campuses across the east coast of Australia (VIC, NSW & QLD), teaches ~2,500 students per annum and is positioned as one of the largest private higher education providers in Australia.

With advanced-design campuses, ongoing technology upgrades, a dedicated team of academics and industry professionals, and a network of international master class lecturers, JMC Academy is committed to ensuring our graduates make their own indelible mark on industry.

Our Purpose

We educate and inspire creative minds to shape their careers and our world

At JMC Academy, we educate and empower creative minds to transform their passion into lifelong careers and meaningful contributions to the world.

As a vibrant, inclusive community of innovators and explorers, we deliver rigorous, real-world education in the creative industries, uniting academic excellence with practical experience, emerging technologies and industry engagement.

We nurture resilience, compassion, and curiosity in every student, helping them chart their unique path, shape their careers, and become creative leaders who inspire, connect, and thrive, locally and globally.

Our Vision

To be the global leader in creative education, chosen by creatives, trusted by industry.

Our Values

Creative Innovators – An opportunity to dream, bring our creative genius to life, making meaningful contribution and delivering innovation to the world stage

Curious & Resilient Explorers – In a world of uncertainly, we are a team of curious minds, who embrace the uncertain, creating a life of learning, exploration and inspiration. At JMC we grow our voice to be confident, and unstoppable. We aren't afraid to fall, as we know we will rise

Inclusive Community of Connectors – We create meaningful pathways, only possible through our connections and impact

Empathetic & Compassionate Leaders – We care about every individual who walks through our doors, with kindness in our hearts and purpose in our minds

Honest, Accountable & of Uncompromising Quality – Our honesty, integrity and quality will not be compromised, as we strive to achieve our best. We are determined to reach our goals, as we hold ourselves and each other accountable

Our Difference

Creative education requires a unique approach. This is why the JMC student experience is different. Our world-class studios, student spaces and hands on learning inspires creative exploration, collaboration and innovation.

Role Summary

The Data & Performance Analyst is the data engine of JMC Academy's Customer Division — providing the intelligence that drives performance across marketing, sales, admissions, onboarding, product, pricing, industry partnerships and alumni engagement.

This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, business intelligence, digital analytics and performance marketing. You will manage data pipelines into our Microsoft Fabric data lakehouse, maintain CRM data quality in HubSpot, build Power BI dashboards, integrate external demand and market intelligence data, drive digital channel performance, and help the entire Customer function make faster, smarter, data-driven decisions.

Technology Environment

HubSpot CRM | Microsoft Fabric | Power BI | SQL | Dynamics 365 ERP | Paradigm SMS | Canvas LMS | VTAC / UAC / QTAC | Burning Glass / Lightcast | Claude AI | Google Analytics 4 | Google Ads | Meta Ads Manager | Microsoft Ads

What You'll Do

Build and maintain data pipelines from Dynamics ERP, Paradigm SMS, Canvas LMS and HubSpot into Microsoft Fabric
Write and maintain SQL queries to extract, transform and load data across JMC's systems and third-party sources
Own digital analytics across GA4, HubSpot and paid media platforms — conversion, attribution and ROAS
Design and maintain Power BI dashboards for marketing, sales, admissions and the CCO
Analyse TAC demand data, DoE datasets and Burning Glass labour intelligence to inform strategy
Build CRM-driven audience segments for activation across Google, Meta and programmatic channels
Embed Claude as an active analytics and decision-support tool across the Customer Division

What We're Looking For

Essential:

3–6 years in data analytics, BI, digital performance or marketing technology
Strong SQL skills — you write queries without assistance
HubSpot CRM configuration, data management and workflow automation experience
Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Factory or Synapse experience
Power BI proficiency including DAX and multi-source data modelling
GA4 and paid media analytics experience (Google, Meta, Microsoft)
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders

Highly regarded:

VTAC, UAC or QTAC tertiary admissions data experience
Burning Glass / Lightcast or LinkedIn Talent Insights
Python or R for advanced analysis and automation
Experience using AI tools — Claude, ChatGPT or similar — for analytics workflows

Why This Role

Technology transformation moment — join as we build our Microsoft Fabric data lakehouse, migrate to HubSpot and embed AI-driven decision making
Extraordinary data breadth — internal CRM, ERP, LMS and SMS unified with TAC demand feeds, DoE datasets and Burning Glass labour intelligence
AI-forward environment — Claude is actively embedded into analytics workflows
Whole-of-function impact — analytics backbone for every team in the Customer Division
Executive visibility — direct reporting line to the Chief Customer Officer
Flexible campus base — Sydney, Melbourne or Brisbane

How to Apply

Please apply with your CV and a brief note telling us:

The most complex data environment you have worked in and how you made sense of it
A specific example of how your analysis changed a business or marketing decision
The tools and platforms you are most proficient in and what you are currently learning

JMC Academy is committed to diversity, equity and inclusion. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures and identities.

Job Title: Principal Engineer / Solutions Architect — AI-Enabled Development (3 Positions)
Ad Text:
We are hiring 3 people into this role. This is a deliberate team build — not a backfill. If you are the right calibre, you are not competing with two other finalists. All three positions are live and we intend to fill them.

The next-generation delivery role you have been waiting for.

The traditional SDLC — BAs working through requirements with business users, writing user stories, developers picking them off a backlog, agile ceremonies — is on its way out at Harry the Hirer. Our business users are already sitting in front of Claude, producing working prototypes themselves. We need a seasoned engineer-architect who can take those prototypes, understand the business outcome & operational context, design and integrate into our platform strategy and technology stack, and turn them into scalable, secure, production software that actually runs the company.

This is not a slide-deck architect role. This is a hands-on coding job as a Principal Engineer / Solutions Architect role for someone who writes code every day, owns the design end-to-end, and sees AI as a skills multiplier — not a threat.

 

About Us

Harry the Hirer is the company behind the infrastructure that makes Australia’s most iconic events happen — the F1 Australian Grand Prix, the Australian Open, and the Melbourne Cup and many more. With over 700 people and a portfolio spanning marquees, flooring, AV, furniture, catering equipment, custom fit-outs and event logistics, we are the operational backbone of the events that put Australia on the global map. We also do exhibitions, private & corporate events and touring/productions.

We are now in an ambitious technology transformation, led by a CTO with a bold vision: a modernised ERP platform built on micro-frontends, modern .NET APIs, event-driven services on Azure, insights and AI driven business processes and AI-assisted platform build from prototypes to production. We are not bolting AI onto a legacy SDLC — we are redesigning the way software is delivered at Harry the Hirer.

 

The Opportunity

You will sit at the intersection of business, architecture and code — partnering directly with the other likewise delivery driven engineers, the engineering leads, business SMEs and executives to expedite the modernisation program across the whole ERP estate.

You will:

Own end-to-end design and delivery of features & subsystems across the modernising ERP platform — Angular micro-frontends, .NET APIs, Azure event-driven services

Work directly with SMEs, operations leaders and executives — interrogating their Claude-generated prototypes, framing the real problem, and translating it into a robust architecture

Use Claude / Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor or likewise as your daily craft — generating, reviewing, refactoring and shipping production code at multiples of traditional velocity

Set the technical direction across multi-team engagements — micro-frontend boundaries, API contracts, event-driven patterns on Azure (Service Bus, Event Grid, APIM, Functions), cloud-first guardrails

Define new ways of working — what replaces the backlog, the standup, the user story when prompts and prototypes become the spec

Mentor or co-invent with the wider engineering group on what “good” looks like when AI is in the loop — prompt discipline, evals, code review, ownership and accountability

Take ownership of the business outcome, not just the commit, the deployment or the ticket count

You will not:

Wait for a written backlog. The prototype, the conversations and the expected outcome are your spec.

Hide behind a process. Your collaboration skills drive the design, planning and code ownership. The opportunity to define the new SDLC processes.

Stop being hands-on. This is a senior coding role with architectural authority — not a job for slideware.

What You Bring

10+ years of solid hands-on commercial coding experience — and you are still writing production code this week

Proven solution architecture track record on multi-team programs — micro-frontends, APIs, eventing, cloud-first design patterns

Deep expertise in Angular/React (modern, micro-frontend ready) and .NET (C#, .NET 8+, ASP.NET Web APIs, EF Core, DDD, Event-Driven, GraphQL)

Strong Azure cloud-native fundamentals — Service Bus, Event Grid, APIM, Functions, App Services / AKS, Azure DevOps, Bicep / Terraform

Demonstrable consulting and facilitation skills — you can workshop with a warehouse supervisor, a CFO and a junior dev in the same hour and leave each one clearer than you found them

Fluent in AI-assisted development — Claude / Claude Code AND GitHub Copilot / Cursor / Windsurf — comfortable with agentic workflows, sub-agents and MCP-style tooling, not just chat

Comfortable working without a traditional Agile backlog — able to define new working agreements with the business when the prototype IS the spec

ERP, logistics or core business platform modernisation experience highly regarded

Australian Citizenship or Permanent Residency, Melbourne-based

 

Why Now?

The industry conversation is about AI replacing developers. We see it differently. The engineers who will define the next decade are seasoned professionals who treat AI as a skills multiplier — and who keep ownership of the code, the design, and the business outcome. Like a farmer with a tractor, not a tourist with a chatbot.

While other tech teams are shrinking, ours is growing — with a CTO who is investing real money, real autonomy and real authority in the people who can show up and ship.

 

What We Offer

•     Permanent FT — salary negotiable for the right person (let’s have the conversation)

•     5 weeks paid annual leave — more than the standard 4

•     Hybrid — 3 office / 2 WFH with flexible daily hours

•     Flexible personal leave — take half your personal days without being sick, no questions asked

•     Free onsite parking at our Abbotsford office

•     Work that actually ships large events, exhibitions, sound & light shows (Productions)

 

How to Maximise Your Chances

We read every application carefully. AI Slop will reduce your chances.

Here is how to stand out:

Personalise your cover letter — tell us specifically why this role, not just any principal engineer and architect role

Tell us about the most recent thing you actually shipped (not designed — shipped) and the outcome it delivered

Address these in your cover letter:

A complex software platform you architected end-to-end — what was the business outcome and what was your specific role?

How are you using AI in your day-to-day coding right now? Be specific — tools, workflows, examples, and what you have learned the hard way.

A time you facilitated a workshop with non-technical SMEs and turned it into a working solution.

 

How to Apply

Click Apply and submit your resume, cover letter, and answers to the screening questions.

Please note: This role is being managed directly by Harry the Hirer. We are not accepting approaches from recruitment agencies or third-party recruiters. Unsolicited candidate submissions will not be considered.

We look forward to meeting you.

Company Overview

Profusion Group is partnering with a truly impressive enterprise that is investing heavily in conversational AI as a core digital servicing channel. This organisation is recognised for strong engineering standards, thoughtful AI governance and a genuine commitment to building safe, trusted and high‑quality AI experiences.

Role Overview

This is a rare opportunity for a Conversational AI Engineer to work on live, production GenAI solutions that sit at the very front of the customer experience.

You’ll own the design, build and operation of enterprise‑grade conversational AI solutions, working closely with Product, Engineering, Architecture, Security and Risk teams.

What you’ll be doing

Building and operating customer‑facing GenAI chatbots across web, portal and mobile
Designing conversational flows, prompts and behaviours that drive real service outcomes
Implementing Retrieval‑Augmented Generation (RAG) using trusted knowledge sources
Integrating with enterprise systems via APIs and secure data patterns
Designing AI‑to‑human escalation into contact centre platforms
Applying content safety, PII redaction and guardrails
Monitoring quality, performance and runtime behaviour in production
Skills & Experience
Proven experience delivering customer‑facing conversational AI or chatbots
Strong understanding of Generative AI, prompt design and orchestration
Hands‑on experience with RAG architectures
Experience using Microsoft Copilot Studio (for conversational AI solutions), Microsoft Dynamics CRM platforms (particularly in servicing contact-centre)
Exposure to the Microsoft ecosystem (Power Platform / Azure)
Comfortable operating in enterprise or regulated environments
Practical, delivery‑focused mindset with strong stakeholder engagement
What’s on Offer
Work on high‑impact GenAI products used by real customers
Strategic, business‑critical role with strong executive visibility
Modern engineering culture with a strong focus on AI safety and quality
Melbourne‑based role with hybrid working
Long term opportunity as conversational AI capability continues to scale
Click APPLY or contact Angela Tan-Jenkin on angela.jenkin@profusiongroup.com for a confidential discussion.
  
Profusion respect people, value diversity and are committed to equality. We are committed to providing a supportive culture and positively contributing towards creating diverse and inclusive workplaces for our candidates & clients. We invite candidates of all ages, genders, sexual orientation, cultural backgrounds, people with disability, neurodiverse individuals, and Indigenous Australians to apply.

Company: Australian Nuclear Science and Technology Organisation
Ad Text:
Company description:



Australian Nuclear Science



Job description:

Position Overview
2 x Ongoing Positions| Full Time | $100k - $134k +15.4% super
Clayton | Work-life balance | Health & Wellbeing Programs
Work at one of Australia's premier scientific facilities, contributing to advanced control and automation systems that enable world-class research. This role offers exposure to cutting-edge technologies at Australian Synchrotron across beamline and accelerator environments, with opportunities for growth aligned to experience and capability.
About the Opportunity

Join the Controls and Scientific Computing function at the Australian Synchrotron, where you will design, develop and support integrated control and automation solutions that enable scientific and operational outcomes. The role focuses on beamline or accelerator systems, including motion control, data acquisition, real-time systems and EPICS-based platforms, working closely with multidisciplinary engineering and science teams.

About You

" We are looking for engineers who are curious, collaborative and capable of working across complex systems. The ideal candidate brings strong technical fundamentals in control or automation systems, with the ability to learn quickly, solve problems and work effectively with cross-functional teams."
- Gaurav Acharya, Controls and Scientific Computing

Your Duties and Responsibilities
Design, develop, implement and support control and automation systems across beamline and accelerator environments
Work with motion control, data acquisition, distributed control and real-time systems
Perform testing, integration and commissioning of hardware and software systems
Troubleshoot and resolve operational issues to maintain reliable system performance
Provide operational support for beamline and accelerator control systems
Support delivery of projects through engineering, integration and commissioning activities
Collaborate with engineering, science and computing teams to deliver integrated solutions
Support system lifecycle activities including upgrades, optimisation and maintenance
Contribute to design reviews, standards and continuous improvement initiatives (depending on experience)

Please click here to view the Position Description.

About Us

ANSTO leverages great science to deliver big outcomes. We partner with scientists and engineers and apply new technologies to provide real-world benefits. Our work improves human health, saves lives, builds our industries and protects the environment. ANSTO is the home of Australia's most significant landmark and national infrastructure for research. Thousands of scientists from industry and academia benefit from gaining access to state-of-the-art instruments every year.


Benefits of Working with Us

ANSTO offers training and development, performance-based salary increases and an extensive range of leave provisions to help employees balance work and personal commitments.

18 days personal/carers leave for each year of service
3 days paid leave between Christmas and New Year

Generous parental leave provisions:

18 weeks Parental leave for primary caregiver
14 weeks Parental leave for secondary caregiver (with incremental increase to a maximum of 18 weeks effective 01/03/2027)
Annual flu vaccinations
Cultural, Naidoc and First Nations ceremonial leave

If you want to join a world class organisation that's making a difference in the lives of Australians, apply now!

How to Apply

For further technical information relating to this position please refer to the Position Description or contact on (03) 8540 5386 . For all other queries please contact Talent Acquisition on +61 (02) 9717 3740.

To be eligible for appointment, applicants will require a security and pre-employment medical assessment.

Application closing date: 10th May 2026 at 11:59pm

All applicants must be Australian citizens.

In order to solve great challenges, we need great minds and great teams. At ANSTO, we understand that diverse teams produce better outcomes and we value the merit that a diverse perspective can bring to our team. We encourage people from diverse backgrounds to apply for our roles including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, primary carers, those with a disability, and women in STEM. ANSTO is a charter member of the Science in Australia Gender Equity (SAGE) initiative and is proudly taking action to create a gender-inclusive workforce.

We've been proudly bringing joy to tables and smiles to faces with the delicious food we've been crafting for over 100 years.  A large part of our strong growth and success is because we're always looking for new ways to do things and thinking about how we can create a better, more sustainable future world together.  Our success as an industry leader in providing deliciously good food means we can continue to invest in our future as we work towards becoming Australia and New Zealand's first choice for poultry.

Your opportunity to join our Technology team in North Ryde!

Inghams is seeking a talented Senior Data Engineer to join our AI, Data & Digital Innovation team and play a foundational role in building enterprise-scale data capabilities that will power our business for the next decade.  This is your opportunity to architect and deliver production-grade data infrastructure at Australia and New Zealand's largest integrated poultry producer.

We're in the midst of an exciting digital transformation journey, rolling out our cloud data platform powered by AWS.  As our Senior Data Engineer, you'll be instrumental in delivering our data transformation initiative—integrating legacy ERP, manufacturing execution, and operational systems with modern cloud architecture, establishing the data foundation that enables AI/ML innovation, consumer insights, and intelligent automation across our operations.

Key responsibilities:

Deliver enterprise-scale data infrastructure at pace using AI-powered development tools to accelerate pipeline development, testing, and deployment cycles, and build production-ready data solutions rapidly through AI-assisted coding, automated testing frameworks, and modern DevOps practices that compress traditional delivery timelines.
Experiment with emerging AWS services, pioneering new architectural patterns, and implementing cutting-edge data engineering techniques.
Build scalable, AI-enabled data pipelines that serve as the foundation for ML/AI innovation, advanced analytics, and intelligent automation. Design data infrastructure that enables rapid experimentation, supports diverse AI/ML workloads, and unlocks new business capabilities across the enterprise.
Rapidly prototype and productionise data solutions that directly impact business outcomes, iterating quickly based on stakeholder feedback and operational metrics.
Champion a culture of rapid innovation and continuous experimentation, leveraging AI-assisted development to compress build cycles, improve code quality, and capture knowledge automatically.
Develop and optimise ETL/ELT processes to integrate legacy ERP, manufacturing execution, and operational systems with modern cloud-based data architecture
Implement data modelling and schema design for AWS services including S3 (data lake), Redshift (data warehouse), and supporting services, applying dimensional modelling and data vault techniques to ensure scalability and performance.
Establish data governance, quality frameworks and data contracts across enterprise data sources
Collaborate with AI, Analytics and BI teams to ensure data availability, discoverability, and fitness for purpose across use cases from operational reporting to advanced ML model training and real-time inference.
Support consumer analytics and customer intelligence capabilities with reliable data infrastructure, APIs, and integration patterns
Work with cross-functional teams to define data requirements, establish data contracts, and deliver against business-driven use cases
Maintain data security, compliance and access controls in line with enterprise governance frameworks and AWS best practices
Monitor, troubleshoot and improve data pipeline performance, reliability and cost-efficiency across the AWS environment, implementing observability, alerting, and automated remediation where appropriate.
Document data architecture, data flows and technical specifications to support knowledge sharing, platform scalability, and onboarding of future team members

Key requirements:

Strong AWS data engineering experience with hands-on expertise in S3, Redshift, Glue, Lambda, Step Functions, Athena, and related services
Production-grade Python and SQL skills with extensive experience building ETL/ELT pipelines, writing complex queries, optimising database performance, and implementing data quality frameworks.
Data modelling and architecture expertise with practical knowledge of dimensional modelling, data vault, schema design patterns, and understanding of how to structure data for both analytical and operational use cases.
Infrastructure as Code (IaC) proficiency using Terraform, CloudFormation, or CDK to provision and manage cloud data infrastructure in a repeatable, version-controlled manner.
CI/CD and DevOps practices with experience implementing automated testing, deployment pipelines, and monitoring for data infrastructure using tools like GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or AWS CodePipeline.
Data governance and quality mindset, understanding the importance of data contracts, lineage, validation, and metadata management
Business translation and communication ability to confidently explain technical data concepts to non-technical stakeholders, gather requirements from business users, and translate ambiguous needs into concrete technical solutions.
Problem-solving and systems thinking with a genuine interest in understanding business challenges, evaluating trade-offs, and delivering practical, maintainable solutions
6–8 years of hands-on data engineering experience in a commercial environment, with at least 3 years focused on cloud data platforms and production pipeline development.
Experience in FMCG, manufacturing, or supply chain environments is advantageous

Overview

This role is focused on building and operating integration services that reduce manual workloads and improve efficiency across systems. While there is opportunity to work with AI-driven and agent-based automation, the primary focus is strong Azure integration delivery within a modern, evolving platform landscape.

Main Responsibilities

Deliver secure, scalable integrations across cloud, SaaS, Microsoft 365, and on-prem systems

Deliver automation solutions that reduce manual effort and improve operational efficiency

Lead position driving AI capability across a federal program , shaping strategy, influencing stakeholders, and leading delivery. 

Key Responsibilities 

Lead design and delivery of AI/ML solutions  
Define data science strategy and best practices  
Mentor junior data scientists  
Engage senior stakeholders across government

Skills

Details 

Location: Canberra  
Contract: 12 months

About the Company

This organisation is a well-established leader in the utilities sector, delivering critical services to a large and growing customer base. With a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, and future-ready technology, they are investing heavily in data, AI, and advanced analytics.




Their technology function is undergoing a major uplift, with AI positioned as a key driver of smarter decision-making, operational efficiency, and long-term value creation. You'll be joining a business where AI is not just experimental-it's becoming embedded across the enterprise.


About the Role

This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling enterprise AI capability. As Lead Data Engineer, you'll design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes across complex operational environments.




You'll work closely with data leaders, architects, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities and bring high-impact AI use cases to life-from predictive models through to cutting-edge generative AI solutions.




Key responsibilities include:





Leading end-to-end AI/ML solution design, development, and deployment
Driving innovation across NLP, LLMs, and advanced ML techniques
Establishing and scaling MLOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, lifecycle management)
Building on modern cloud and data platforms (Azure, Databricks)
Embedding AI into enterprise systems to improve efficiency and decision-making
Championing governance, best practice, and responsible AI frameworks
Mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional delivery teams

This role offers a strong mix of strategy and hands-on engineering, with real influence over how AI is adopted across the organisation.


About You

You're an experienced AI/ML engineer with a strong track record of delivering production-ready solutions at scale. You're comfortable operating across both technical and business domains and enjoy turning complex ideas into practical outcomes.




You'll bring:





Strong background in software or data engineering (Python preferred)
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and Databricks ecosystem
Deep experience with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn)
Solid MLOps capability, including model deployment and monitoring
Exposure to generative AI, LLMs, and modern AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain)
Proven ability to deliver AI solutions that drive measurable business value
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership experience

Ad Text:
Are you a cloud infrastructure leader ready to shape enterprise-scale digital platforms that enable innovation, reliability and security?

Deakin is seeking a Principal, Cloud Infrastructure Engineering to provide specialist technical leadership across our cloud environments, ensuring our infrastructure is resilient, scalable and designed to support the future of digital, AI and data-enabled services.

Based at either our Melbourne Burwood or Geelong Waterfront Campus, hybrid work available 
Fulltime and continuing
Competitive package

Why work with us?

At Deakin University, you’ll find more than a job - you’ll find a community and a way of working that supports you to do your best work. Across our campuses in Geelong, Melbourne and regional Victoria, we offer an environment designed to help you thrive, with on-site childcare, gyms, medical centres, cafés, accessible public transport and convenient parking.

We support flexible and hybrid working, generous leave provisions - including extended parental leave and the option to purchase additional leave - and a wide range of staff benefits and discounts. Combined with our strong focus on health, wellbeing and professional development, Deakin offers a supportive, inclusive culture where leaders are trusted, people are valued and careers can grow.

About the role

Deakin isn't just adopting AI – we're building it. With Deakin GEM now live and our AI Factory, a multi-disciplinary squad, accelerating capability across the institution, ambitious work is underway across multiple streams.

As Principal, Cloud Engineering Infrastructure you will be in a hands-on technical role, working with Data, Engineering and Product specialists to provide the high-performing, cost-efficient and secure environments that make our AI concepts a reality. You will also collaborate with peers in the wider Cloud Systems & Services team to embed shared best-practices for automated cloud infrastructure management. You will be the technical authority who defines and implements the scalable, production-grade cloud infrastructure that supports our GEM Generative AI solutions and agentic workflows on AWS. You should be comfortable delivering complex Infrastructure as Code deployments, establishing and managing effective enterprise-grade observability, and in making robust long-term architectural decisions.

You will:

Collaborate with stakeholders to align technical roadmaps with business goals.
Architect end-to-end cloud environments specifically optimized for AI/ML workloads.
Write production-ready IaC (Terraform) for auto-scaling GPU and CPU clusters.
Design CI/CD pipelines that automate the deployment of both infrastructure and ML models.
Design and implement comprehensive observability and monitoring capabilities
Optimize cloud spend through proactive monitoring and architectural refinements.

About you

You are a highly experienced cloud infrastructure engineering professional who thrives in complex enterprise environments.

You’ll bring:

Expert-level AWS AI skills: Deep knowledge of Bedrock, Lambda, EventBridge, SQS, and EKS & ECS Fargate.
Infrastructure as Code: High proficiency in Terraform and AWS CDK.
Data Engineering: Experience RDS and with RAG vector databases.
Security: Solid understanding of IAM roles, VPC security, and data resilience (encryption, backup, redundancy) at scale.
Operations: Mastery of MLOps principles, including model monitoring and drift detection, as well as core platform metric observability.

For a copy of the position description, please see below:

PD - Principal, Cloud Infrastructure Engineering.pdf

 

To apply

Please submit your updated resume and a short cover letter outlining your skills and experience.

For a confidential discussion regarding this role, please contact Stephen Castellas – Director, Digital Infrastructure & Cloud Services via stephen.castellas@deakin.edu.au 

Applications close Sunday, 17 May 2026 at 11:55pm.

 

Are You Ready?

Deakin is a Victorian university with a global impact. We are an agile, dynamic, and innovative university committed to making a positive impact through our excellence in education, research and innovation and the contributions we make to the wider community.

We understand that our reputation has been built on the dedication and expertise of our staff and we offer a dynamic and diverse working environment with opportunities to grow and develop careers. We believe that a progressive, thriving culture will ensure that people choose to come, and stay at Deakin and contribute to our ongoing success.

We value diversity and aim to build an inclusive environment that champions, embraces and respects differences. We support and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, and people of all abilities, cultures, sexual orientation, and genders.

 

Pre-employment safety and suitability checks: In accordance with the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Respond to Gender-based Violence, appointment to this role is subject to successful completion of relevant pre-employment checks, including Working With Children Checks and candidate gender-based violence declarations. 

Candidates may be asked to declare whether they have been investigated for an allegation of Gender‑based Violence, or determined to have engaged in conduct that constitutes Gender‑based Violence during the course of their previous employment, or otherwise in a legal process.  A declaration is not required from individuals who have experienced gender‑based violence.  A ‘Yes’ response does not automatically exclude a candidate from employment, and any information provided will be treated confidentially and considered only for relevance to the role and the University’s safety obligations.

About us

Southern Launch is a place where curiosity, responsibility and ambition meet to deliver space mission success for our customers. We are bold, we are brave and we are building infrastructure to take technology to space…and back!

Since 2018 we have been pushing the boundaries of what is possible and achieving firsts for Australia and the world!

Our team thrives on solving hard problems, backing each other, and taking pride in work that genuinely matters. We move fast, think big and take responsibility, for our work, our customers and the communities we operate in.

At Southern Launch, you’re trusted to take ownership, encouraged to think differently, and supported by people who are grounded, capable and quietly determined. If you’re driven by impact, adventure and purpose, you’ll fit right in.

Come and help us shape the future of what’s possible, both here on Earth and beyond.

Model, Simulation and Analysis Engineer

We are seeking an experienced Model, Simulation, and Analysis Engineer who can bring proven capability to our mission development and flight safety programs. You will play a key role in shaping mission profiles, assessing flight risk, and supporting safe and successful launch and return operations. This is a hands-on technical role suited to someone who has already applied these skills in an operational, aerospace, defence, or high-integrity engineering environment.

Responsibilities

Mission development and analysis: trajectory optimisation, mission planning, operational driver assessments, and the development and refinement of mission analysis tools. 

Flight safety: statistical modelling, failure analysis, risk modelling, and the development of flight safety software and mapping tools. 

Launch engineering: meteorological assessment, situational awareness tools, range software development, and support for launch operations. 

Launch vehicle model verification: 6-DOF modelling, aerodynamic and mass property development, and validation of end-to-end vehicle performance. 

About you

We value the diverse experience within our team and are seeking someone who can immediately contribute with their existing expertise. We are looking for: 

Bachelor of Engineering (Mechanical, Mechatronic, Aerospace, or equivalent). 

Demonstrated experience applying simulation, modelling, and analysis in a complex or safety-critical environment (aerospace, defence, space, robotics, etc.). 

Strong proficiency in mathematics, statistics, and flight mechanics. 

Advanced MATLAB/Simulink skills, with working knowledge of C++ or Python. 

Experience in model-based design and object-oriented development principles. 

Ability to collaborate effectively while operating independently in a fast-paced technical setting. 

An Australian Citizen eligible for NV1 security clearance.

What We Offer at Southern Launch

Flexibility
We operate a genuinely flexible work culture with a mix flexibility for school runs, exercise, appointments and life outside work.

Collaboration
You’ll work in a small, capable team where ideas are easy to share, discussions are direct, and you have clear access to leaders and decision‑makers.

Meaningful work
You’ll have clear line‑of‑sight between your work and real mission, customer and business outcomes, not just a small task buried in a large organisation.

Professional development
You’ll receive $2,500 per financial year to support training, certifications and learning aligned to your role and career development goals.

Share Options
Eligible roles may receive equity grants through our Employee Share Option Plan (ESOP), allowing you to share in the long‑term success of the business.

Wellbeing support
All employees have access to confidential psychological support through our Employee Assistance Program, with professional, external counsellors available when needed.

Challenging projects
No two days are the same, from launch campaigns and infrastructure development to customer missions and regulatory environments, your work will remain diverse and engaging.

Mission critical environments
You’ll gain hands‑on experience working in defence and space adjacent environments!

How to Apply

If this sounds like the job for you it’s time to hit the launch button and apply with your resume and cover letter.

We encourage you to apply as soon as possible, as applications will be assessed on a rolling basis and the role may close early.

Who We Are

Akkodis, we thrive on solving complex problems and building a smarter future; we power digital transformation and accelerate innovation with our cross-industry technology and digital engineering consulting, talent services and skilling. We're passionate about our work and committed to creating and implementing cutting-edge solutions that drive industry progress. Our culture is built on honesty, respect, fairness and inclusion, and we're looking for innovative and talented individuals who share these values to join us on this journey.



The Role

Akkodis is seeking an experienced Data Scientist to join a high-performing delivery team building and operationalising enterprise AI capabilities on a modern cloud-based data platform. You'll work hands-on across the end-to-end lifecycle - problem framing, data exploration, model development, deployment, and ongoing support - delivering AI solutions that are production-ready, governed, and aligned to business outcomes.



As a Data Scientist, you will design, build, deploy and support AI/ML solutions using Microsoft Azure services and a lakehouse-style data platform. The environment is modern, cloud-first, and focused on scaling AI capability across the enterprise.



Please note this role is based onsite in Perth, and cannot be performed as WFH or remotely. The contract is for 12 months plus potential extensions.



Key Responsibilities

Design and develop AI/ML solutions aligned to business requirements within Microsoft Azure
Write and maintain Python code for data preparation, analysis and modelling
Build predictive models, perform statistical analysis, and apply descriptive/diagnostic/predictive/prescriptive methods
Collaborate with stakeholders, analysts and engineers to identify opportunities and shape AI solutions
Contribute to experimentation (hypothesis testing, model evaluation, iteration) to improve outcomes
Develop and maintain CI/CD (DevOps) pipelines for sustainable, frequent deployment
Work effectively in a hybrid team (multi-vendor and in-house) and align to agreed technical standards and ways of working
Produce clear technical documentation (logical/physical design, implementation notes, standards)
Support stakeholder engagement, issue resolution, and contribute to risk/issue management




Required Skills & Experience

Strong capability in Python, R and SQL for data manipulation, modelling and visualisation
Demonstrated experience delivering ML/AI solutions into production environments
Experience working closely with Business Analysts and Data Engineers to locate suitable data sources and map data to requirements
Proven analytical and problem-solving skills with strong delivery discipline
Strong written communication and documentation skills, including technical design documentation
Ability to influence stakeholders and provide specialist advice
5+ years relevant experience in a Data Scientist / applied AI role



Technology Environment

You'll work in a Microsoft-first cloud environment, with services such as:

Azure AI Foundry and Azure AI services (Search, Translator, Speech, Language, Vision, Content)
Azure Fabric Data Science, Azure SQL, Azure Cosmos
Modern data platform patterns (lakehouse-style delivery)
DevOps practices including CI/CD pipelines for deployment and ongoing support



Qualifications

Tertiary qualifications in IT, Computer Science, Data Science or a related discipline
Industry certification (highly regarded), e.g., Azure Data Scientist Associate



Security & Compliance Requirement

This role supports a high-trust environment. You must be willing and able to meet pre-engagement screening requirements, including eligibility to obtain and maintain an appropriate government security clearance and compliance with confidentiality obligations.



Talented, committed, can-do individuals have always been the cornerstone of our dynamic organisation. If you have what it takes to thrive in this role and want to know more, please reach out to Steve at steve.cook@akkodisgroup.com



Why Work for Us?

Joining Akkodis means being part of a dynamic community of engineers that's at the forefront of tech innovation. You'll have the opportunity to work with cutting-edge technologies:

Whether your interests lie in systems engineering, software development, data platforms or any other tech discipline, we have a role that will suit you. You'll get to collaborate with talented expert colleagues from around the world who share your enthusiasm and passion for technology. We offer a flexible and supportive work environment, and we're committed to helping you grow and achieve your career goals. If you want to make a real difference, Akkodis is the place for you.

Akkodis is a commercial brand under which both AKKA and Modis entities operate.



"Akkodis is an Equal Opportunity employer, committed to all forms of workforce inclusion. We actively invite and encourage applications by candidates from diverse backgrounds".

About Us

Adaptiv is a true Kiwi success story! Recognised for our commitment to premium client delivery, we provide world-class integration, AI consulting and development service, along with data and analytics. We have a wealth of development and integration experience in moving clients from around the world onto cloud platforms.




Our integration work focuses on the exciting challenge of designing innovative API-led and event driven solutions. Leveraging the latest technologies, such as Databricks, MuleSoft, Boomi, Azure, and Solace, we develop services that unlock access to existing data within legacy business systems in new and ingenious ways.




These solutions enable organizations to drive major process efficiencies, engineer new operational workflows, and extract deeper insights from their data. Ultimately, this empowers businesses to operate more effectively and engage with their customers in modern, cutting-edge ways. Our services also include cloud consultancy, round-the clock middleware support, integration development, application lifecycle management, load and performance testing and customised integration training.




Based in our stylish Southbank offices in Melbourne, our team enjoys a collaborative, people-first culture where curiosity, initiative, and growth are valued. We combine serious technical capability with a down-to-earth, can-do approach and we’re looking for someone who shares that mindset.





About the Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Senior Data Engineer / Consultant to join our growing Data Engineering practice here at Adaptiv Integration to drive the delivery of data engineering engagements on behalf of our clients. In this role, you will ensure high-quality technical outcomes, foster strong client collaboration, and provide effective support to delivery teams.




This position combines advanced data engineering expertise with consulting leadership. You will own engineering delivery across assigned engagements, contribute to solution design within architectural guidelines, and mentor junior and intermediate team members to maintain consistent quality and standards.




This role will be responsible for the delivery of cutting-edge Databricks technology, driving innovative data engineering solutions for clients. You will leverage your expertise to design, implement, and optimise Databricks-based pipelines and solutions, ensuring high-quality technical outcomes and efficient, scalable data architectures.




The role is highly hands-on and delivery-focused, requiring autonomous execution and a strong emphasis on billable contribution.




What You’ll Be Doing

Build, maintain, and optimise data pipelines across lakes, lakehouses, and warehouses.
Write, test, and maintain high-quality code; develop transformations for reporting, analytics, and AI/ML.
Apply data modelling principles; troubleshoot and resolve issues efficiently.
Work with Databricks, Delta Live Tables, Lakeflow, Git, and CI/CD pipelines.
Implement automated testing, data validation, and performance optimisation.
Lead unit and integration testing; ensure solutions meet quality and documentation standards.
Support knowledge sharing and improvement of internal frameworks and templates.
Act as primary engineering contact, leading technical discussions and translating business needs into technical tasks.
Communicate progress, risks, and blockers; contribute to estimation, scoping, and planning.
Mentor and coach junior/intermediate team members; conduct peer reviews.
Ensure accountability for code quality, documentation, and adherence to best practices.
Collaborate with architects on technical solutions and delivery planning.
Participate in learning sessions and knowledge sharing; adopt modern data concepts (AI/ML, data visualisation, cloud-native).
Contribute to process, template, and documentation improvements; pursue relevant training and certification.




What You’ll Bring

6+ years of professional experience in data engineering or a related field.

At least 4 years of hands-on experience developing data pipelines and transformations using Databricks, including Delta Live Tables, notebooks, and workflows.

Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric concepts and experience contributing to projects that incorporate Fabric components.

Proficiency in Python and SQL for data transformation and analysis.
Understanding of data modelling including Star and Snowflake schema design for analytics workloads.
Knowledge of Medallion architecture principles and well-architected, scalable data solution design.

Experience applying DevOps practices such as version control, CI/CD, and environment automation.

Experience with testing and data quality frameworks to ensure reliable, accurate data delivery.

Experience with visualisation and data consumption concepts, including Power BI or similar tools.


Relevant industry certifications (Databricks Professional and Databricks Associate) or a demonstrated commitment to ongoing professional development.

 

If you are looking for your next role with an organisation where you can elevate your career, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

Ad Text:
AI Systems & Automation Developer

Grove is looking for an AI Systems & Automation Developer to help build the internal tools, workflows and technical infrastructure that will support the next stage of our business.

This is a hands-on development role for a technically capable graduate, early-career developer or systems-minded builder who can take clear business objectives and turn them into practical, scalable internal tools.

We know the business problems we want to solve. We are looking for someone who can design the technical infrastructure and build the tools to solve them.

This role will work across estimating, design, procurement, production, resource planning and project delivery, developing internal systems that improve speed, accuracy, visibility and consistency across the business.

This is an opportunity to help build Grove’s internal AI and automation capability from the ground up.




What you’ll be doing

Understanding business objectives set by senior management and translating them into technical solutions.

Designing the internal architecture required to support AI, automation and internal tool development.

Selecting appropriate development environments, platforms, APIs, databases and workflow tools.

Building internal tools across estimating, design, procurement, production, resource planning and project delivery.

Developing AI-assisted workflows using tools such as OpenAI, Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Replit, Retool or similar platforms.

Creating practical tools that improve speed, accuracy, consistency and visibility.

Building prototypes quickly, testing them with users, and refining them into reliable internal systems.

Structuring data and workflows so tools can be maintained, reused and scaled.

Developing dashboards, internal applications, automations and reporting tools.

Documenting the architecture, logic, workflows and user instructions for each tool developed.

Skills and experience

The ideal candidate will have:

Coding, software development or automation experience.

Ability to use AI-assisted coding tools to build working tools or applications.

Strong understanding of how to structure workflows, data and systems.

Ability to translate business objectives into technical requirements.

Ability to work independently and make technical recommendations.

Strong communication skills with non-technical users.

Experience with any of the following would be highly regarded:

Python, JavaScript, SQL, APIs or database tools.

OpenAI, Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Replit, Retool, Make, Zapier or similar.

ERP systems, spreadsheets, project management tools or document processing.

AI agents, structured prompts, document automation and internal business systems.




What success looks like

Grove has a clear internal architecture for AI and automation.

Internal tools are built in a structured, scalable way.

Business objectives from senior management are converted into working systems, in a timely fashion.

Tools are adopted by estimating, design, procurement, production and planning teams.

Accuracy, visibility and consistency improve across key workflows.

Systems are documented and capable of being expanded over time.

About Grove

Grove is a privately owned Australian business with operations across modular construction, hire, transport, property and motorsport.

We are continuing to invest in better systems, stronger processes and more efficient ways of working across the group. This role will sit at the intersection of technology and operations, building tools that directly impact how the business estimates, designs, procures, manufactures, plans and delivers work.

For the right person, this is a rare opportunity to build an internal AI and automation function from the ground up inside a large, practical and fast-moving business.

AI Engineer
LK Property | Melbourne, VIC

Most AI ‘engineers’ write strategies. This role builds the thing.

LK Group is one of Australia’s most ambitious diversified groups — spanning the NBL, luxury property, premium retail, hospitality, and technology. We’ve just formed a dedicated AI team with a clear mandate: move fast, build well, and create measurable value across our portfolio. This is the role that makes that happen.
  
You’re not here to advise. You’re here to ship. If you thrive when you’re building real systems that go live and create outcomes — and you want to do that at scale, with autonomy, inside a group that genuinely backs its people — keep reading.
  
About Us

Few groups move as fast or think as broadly as LK Group. Our businesses range from the National Basketball League, to major retailers such as Daniel’s Donuts, luxury property development, HR and recruitment, exciting digital and technology startups and much more. We aspire to achieve success and professionalism throughout our established subsidiary companies, and within our accomplished and integrated team, to deliver an exceptional experience to clients and businesses. Today, the Group employs thousands of people across the world who are truly committed to those values.
  
The Role

As our founding AI Engineer, you’re a core member of a newly formed AI Value Creation team, reporting to the GM AI Value Creation. Your job is to design, build, and deploy AI agents and automation pipelines that save real time across LK Group’s Support Team and Brand Portfolio — and then keep building.

Day to day, you’ll be:

Designing and deploying AI agents natively within Anthropic’s Claude platform
Integrating AI services into existing business systems via APIs, webhooks, and data pipelines
Building and maintaining automated workflow pipelines using n8n, Zapier, Make, and Shopify Flow
Testing agents against real business scenarios and resolving edge cases before they go live
Monitoring agent performance — reliability, latency, error rates — and keeping the standard high
Building and maintaining a library of reusable prompt templates, workflows, and system instructions
Writing clear technical documentation — README files, runbooks, integration guides — that others can actually follow
Supporting responsible AI practices across every build — data privacy, accuracy, and quality control embedded from the start  
This is a founding role. The infrastructure you build will define how AI gets done at LK Group.

Who We’re Looking For

We want a builder — someone who comes alive when they’re shipping, thinks in pipelines and agents, and has the engineering rigour to make things reliable and built to last.
  
You’re the right fit if you:

Have proven, hands-on experience building and deploying AI systems — what you’ve shipped matters more than how long you’ve been doing it
Have production experience integrating large language models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Copilot) into real applications — not just consumer or chat use
Are experienced with prompt engineering and LLM configuration: system prompts, tool use, multi-step chains, and output evaluation
Have built production workflows with n8n, Make, Zapier, or equivalent — things that actually work in production, not just prototypes
Have strong working knowledge of REST APIs, webhooks, authentication patterns, and data pipelines
Exercise strong engineering judgement — you choose the right tool, scope builds pragmatically, and debug systematically

Job Title: Software Services AI Solutions Engineer II
